title: 1865: The Iron Mountain Railroad Expansion
author: Historical Fact
content: Following the Civil War, the Iron Mountain Railroad was pushed through the rugged Ozark terrain. It didn't just move people; it enabled the mass extraction of timber and mineral resources that were previously inaccessible.
action: Action: What 'transportation' layer are you missing that prevents you from moving your 'resources' (data/results) to their destination?

title: 1870-1880: The Sawmill Boom
author: Historical Fact
content: In 1870, 23 sawmills in the southeast Ozark region produced 5.5 million board feet of lumber. By 1880, that number rose to 47 mills producing over 40 million board feet.
action: Action: When you see a high-growth phase in your project, audit the 'waste' (chat piles/sawdust). Are you moving so fast you’re ignoring the byproducts?

title: 1890: The Shift in Migration Origins
author: Historical Fact
content: Before 1860, settlers came primarily from Kentucky, Tennessee, and Virginia. By 1890, migrants from Illinois, Indiana, and Ohio were arriving in numbers that equaled or exceeded the Appalachian contingent.
action: Action: Audit your sources of information. Are you only looking at the same 'neighborhoods' (sources/websites) you always have? Try a new perspective.

title: 1900: The Era of 'Squatter' Homesteading
author: Historical Fact
content: Many Ozark settlers arrived with minimal capital, squatting on federal land, improving it, and hoping to eventually buy it. This created a culture of extreme self-reliance and suspicion of outside authority.
action: Action: Identify a part of your process that is 'squatted'—a hacky workaround you’re doing for now. When will you 'formalize' it?

title: 1960: The Retirement Migration
author: Historical Fact
content: Starting in the 1960s, the Ozarks experienced a new migration wave, not of laborers, but of retirees from the Midwest, leading to the creation of planned communities and a new service-based economy.
action: Action: How has your project's 'audience' changed since you started? Adjust your output to match your current stakeholders.
